zh.lifeSummary: 當Dorothy Barber於1577年出生在英國素福爾可易普斯維其時，她的父親Thomas Barber28歲，母親Eleanor29歲。她於1609年與Joseph Morse在英國素福爾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1671年3月4日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利享年94歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dorothy Barber于1577年出生在英国素福爾可易普斯維其时，她的父亲Thomas Barber28岁，母亲Eleanor29岁。她于1609年与Joseph Morse在英国素福爾可结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1671年3月4日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利享年94岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barber
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a barber, from Middle English barb(o)ur ‘barber’ (Anglo-Norman French barber, Old French barbier, from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). In the Middle Ages barbers not only cut hair and shaved beards, but also practised surgery and pulled teeth.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name from German Barbier ‘barber’ (compare 1 above).
Catalan: occupational name for a barber, barber (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barbier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Emile, Pierre, Alexandre, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Laurette, Manon, Marcel, Marcellin, Michel.
French: occupational name for a barber-surgeon (see Barber ), Old French barbier (from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’). Compare Barbe and Barbee .
German: occupational name for a barber, from Middle High German barbierer, or an occupational name for an armorer, from barbier(e) ‘metal visor (to protect the face)’.

variants.name: Berber
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Salvador, Guillermo, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Luis, Adriana, Alfredo, Angelina, Armando, Carlos.
Catalan: probably a variant of   Barber  . This surname is most common in Mexico.
Turkish: occupational name from berber ‘barber’.

variants.name: Barbero
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ricardo, Alvino, Ana, Angel, Arsenio, Fernando, Gustavo, Jaime, Jose, Maribel, Mario, Nenita.  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Domenic, Ernani, Giulio, Italo.  French Emile, Yves.

Spanish and Italian (Piedmont): occupational name for a barber-surgeon, barbero, from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’ (from Latin barba); see also Barber . This surname is also found in southern France.

variants.name: Barbieri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Salvatore, Angelo, Vito, Rocco, Aldo, Cosmo, Dino, Domenic, Geno, Giovanna, Guido.
Italian: occupational name from barbiere ‘barber-surgeon’ (see Barber ), from Late Latin barbarius, a derivative of barba ‘beard’.

variants.name: Barbour
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: occupational name from Old French barbeor ‘barber’. Compare the English form Barber .

History: George Barbour sailed from England on the Transport in 1635. He became a freeman of Dedham, MA, in 1647 and settled eventually at Medfield. A Scottish merchant named Barbour settled in eastern VA in the latter half of the 17th century.

variants.name: Borbely
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Antal, Ferenc, Ildiko, Kalman, Laszlo, Miklos.
Hungarian (Borbély): occupational name for a barber, Hungarian borbély. See also Barber .

variants.name: Barbeau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Monique, Alcide, Alphonse, Amiee, Armand, Gisele, Jean Francois, Lucien, Lucienne, Michel.
French:

from barbeau ‘barbel’, a type of fish, hence a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, or a nickname for a man with a sparse beard, the fish being distinguished by beardlike growths on either side of its mouth.

nameMeanings.name: Dorothy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothee
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothie
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Dorothy Barber was born in 1577, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, her father, Thomas Barber, was 28 and her mother, Eleanor, was 29. She married Joseph Morse in 1609, in Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 4 March 1671, in Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 94.

ko.Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 왓르타운
ko.lifeSummary: Dorothy Barber(이)가 1577년 영국 수폴크 이프스위치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Barber(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1609년 영국 수폴크에서 Joseph Morse(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1671년 3월 4일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이에서 94세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Dorothy Barber nasceu em 1577, em Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Barber, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Joseph Morse em 1609, em Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 4 de março de 1671, em Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 94 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dorothy Barber родилась в 1577 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Barber было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Joseph Morse в 1609 г. в местности суффолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 4 марта 1671 г. в местности судбури, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 94 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Dorothy Barber nació en 1577, en Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Barber, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Eleanor, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Joseph Morse en 1609, en Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 4 de marzo de 1671, en Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 94 años.
